what_it_does: This file runs a static, non-spawning regression and self-consistency gate over spawn-flag posture classifications derived from the Mirror-Registry rules for the claude-code platform. It compares registry-derived postures for every key in SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY against a frozen curated ground-truth map, verifies each flag classifies to a real posture rather than unmatched, and returns a structured report with any divergences.
observations: Contains a frozen `_EXPECTED_POSTURE` dictionary with 48 spawn-flag keys mapped to postures.
Performs pure static classification and never spawns a model or calls Claude.
Imports `SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY` and `SPAWN_FLAG_BODY_KEY_MAP` inside `crosscheck()` at call time.
Uses `rules.classify(flag, signal_sets)` to derive each flag's posture from the platform registry.
Checks both posture agreement and that the derived posture is one of locked/hazard/consent/safe, treating `unmatched` as a contradiction.
Detects missing or stale expectation keys when the assembly table drifts relative to the frozen map.
`main()` prints a human-readable summary and exits with code 1 on any divergence.
Docstring documents a historical role change from F-FIX-5 gate to F-FIX-9 post-swap regression gate.
standouts: It is a regression oracle: a frozen 48/48 zero-divergence snapshot guards against silent rule drift after the hand-maintained posture column was deleted.
It fails loudly on any posture mismatch, unmatched classification, or unrecorded new spawn-flag key.
It exports `crosscheck()` so other acceptance tests can call the same gate without duplicating logic.
conventions: Uses a long module docstring to record history, role change, and fixture semantics.
Uppercase private constants (`_EXPECTED_POSTURE`, `_REAL_POSTURES`) for frozen test data.
Late imports inside `crosscheck()` to avoid import-time coupling and support callers without the supervisor context.
Structured report dict with explicit `ok`, `agreements`, and `divergences` fields.
`main()` returns an integer exit code rather than raising directly.
concerns: The frozen expectation map must be manually updated whenever a spawn flag is added or removed; forgetting to do so triggers a loud failure.
Default `platform_id='claude-code'` is hardcoded, so reuse for other platforms requires an explicit argument.
Late imports mean missing dependencies surface only when `crosscheck()` is called, not at module import.
The file references R6-corrected SignalSets and F-FIX milestones without defining them locally.
A `noqa: F401` comment acknowledges an import used only for side-effect registration.
notes: A frozen regression gate that verifies every spawn-flag's registry-derived posture still matches the 48/48 verified snapshot and never classifies as unmatched.
questions: What exact signal-set features and rule axes does `rules.classify` use to assign each posture?
Where is `SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY` defined and what fields does each row contain besides `flag`?
How is `SPAWN_FLAG_BODY_KEY_MAP` populated and kept in sync with the assembly table?
What do the F-FIX-5 and F-FIX-9 identifiers refer to in the broader project roadmap?
What is the R6 correction to transport_invariants mentioned in the comments?
What is the exact return shape of `rules.classify` beyond the three unpacked values?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is exactly the post-deletion regression and self-consistency gate it claims to be, checking frozen expectations, real postures, and key coverage.
apparent_themes: Acceptance-test reuse via exported gate function
Documenting historical design decisions in code
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent silent posture regressions after removing the hand-written SPAWN_FLAGS posture column by freezing the verified snapshot and making any rule or signal-set drift fail loudly.
novelty: It embeds a detailed historical role-change narrative in the module docstring and uses a manually curated frozen posture map as a long-lived regression oracle, treating an `unmatched` classification as a logical contradiction for a declared assemblable flag.
contribution: A reusable `crosscheck()` function and standalone `main()` that provide a structured, loud-failing regression report for spawn-flag postures, preserving the verified snapshot and guarding against unrecorded key additions or rule drift.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test that statically cross-checks registry-derived spawn-flag postures against a frozen 48-entry ground-truth map and reports divergences without spawning any model.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a standing regression and self-consistency gate ensuring the Mirror-Registry remains the sole source of spawn-flag posture truth after the hand-maintained SPAWN_FLAGS dict was deleted.

symbols.does: Runs the post-deletion regression and self-consistency gate over spawn-flag postures and returns a structured report dict.
symbols.description: This function imports the spawn-flag assembly and body-key map at call time, retrieves the platform's signal sets from the registry, classifies each flag using `rules.classify`, compares the derived posture to the frozen `_EXPECTED_POSTURE` map, checks that the posture is real rather than unmatched, detects missing or stale keys, and returns a report with agreements and divergences.

symbols.does: Executes `crosscheck()` and prints a human-readable summary, exiting with 1 if any divergences exist.
symbols.description: This entrypoint prints the platform id, number of keys checked, agreement counts, posture breakdown, and detailed reasons for each divergence; it returns 0 only when every spawn-flag posture matches the frozen ground truth and every key classifies to a real posture.
